skill.name: project-planner
skill.description: Detects stale project plans and suggests session commands for saving, syncing, and reviewing docs/PLAN.md.
skill.summary: Keep docs/PLAN.md current by detecting stale plans and suggesting planning commands.
skill.author: 0xDarkMatter

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backtick references as Ruby or shell execution, but SKILL.md contains no executable code or command runner. The weak cryptography alert is a keyword false positive in frontmatter text, and no prompt injection or exfiltration behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Project plans often drift away from real work as commits and sessions continue. This skill detects stale docs/PLAN.md state and suggests the right save or sync action.
actual_capabilities: Checks whether docs/PLAN.md exists or is missing.
Identifies plans that may be stale compared with recent git activity.
Suggests save and sync commands for common planning situations.
Recognizes uncommitted work that may require plan review.
Supports session-start reminders to restore project context.
limitations: Does not directly create, edit, or archive plan files.
Depends on separate save and sync commands for actual plan operations.
Uses simple staleness heuristics instead of full project management logic.
Does not validate whether plan content matches every source change.

use_cases.title: Resume a Development Session
use_cases.description: Detect an existing docs/PLAN.md and suggest syncing project state before work continues.

use_cases.title: Review Progress After Commits
use_cases.description: Notice when commits have landed after the plan was last updated and suggest a plan refresh.

use_cases.title: Create Planning Discipline
use_cases.description: Prompt teams to save goals, progress, and completion notes during active project work.

prompt_templates.title: Check Plan Status
prompt_templates.prompt: Check whether this project has a current docs/PLAN.md and tell me what planning action to take next.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check at the start of a session

prompt_templates.title: Sync Recent Progress
prompt_templates.prompt: Review recent project activity against docs/PLAN.md and suggest whether I should sync from git or status.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Stale Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the last plan update with recent commits and uncommitted work. Recommend the best save or sync command.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Maintenance Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate plan freshness, session context, and current work state. Give a concise maintenance sequence for docs/PLAN.md.

output_examples.input: I just reopened this repository. What should I do with the project plan?
output_examples.output: The project has an existing docs/PLAN.md. Restore context first, then review whether recent work requires a plan update.

output_examples.input: The plan was updated five days ago and several commits landed since then.
output_examples.output: The plan may be stale. Sync from recent git history, then save any new decisions from the current session.

output_examples.input: There is no docs/PLAN.md yet, but we discussed project goals.
output_examples.output: Create the initial plan from the current goals so future sessions have a shared project reference.

best_practices: Run a plan status check when starting or resuming project work.
Refresh the plan after meaningful commits, completed tasks, or direction changes.
Keep docs/PLAN.md focused on current goals, progress, blockers, and next actions.
anti_patterns: Treating the skill as a full project management system.
Ignoring stale plan warnings after major implementation changes.
Using planning commands without first clarifying the current project state.

faq.question: Does this skill edit docs/PLAN.md directly?
faq.answer: No. It only suggests when to use save or sync commands.

faq.question: What file does it focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on docs/PLAN.md as the project planning source of truth.

faq.question: How does it detect a stale plan?
faq.answer: It compares plan age, recent git activity, and signs of active project progress.

faq.question: Can it replace a task tracker?
faq.answer: No. It is a lightweight session planning helper, not a task database.

faq.question: Does it require network access?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file does not define network behavior.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
